instruction: Use Sefer memory and runtime kernels only.
Report what theta_axis10_residual_star is from carried evidence.
Reveal only properties emitted by Themis or newly computed by the runtime.
Do not infer biological meaning from Chat language.
Do not translate unknown into known vocabulary.
If a property is hidden, emit it as hidden/not surfaced/unknown with path and reason.
If a property is measurable, emit the value, source path, atom, and seal.
If a property requires deeper read-depth, emit the next read-depth probe instead of guessing.

card.ask: Using Sefer evidence and runtime kernels only, explain what C_a8 closure residual is, which atoms feed it, what natural law path could reduce C_a8 toward zero without forcing closure, and what next probe should be run.
card.spoken:
C_a8 NATURAL LAW PATH — SPOKEN KERNEL RESULT
============================================================

The runtime accepted the C_a8 question and judged it.

Judgment:
  status: computed
  seal:   6d6a09d87f63b408a17af164f53925345dd7af84cb2d94ee9cf7cbff4cfe704d

Runtime:
  records_read:    1000
  cycles_used:     2
  express_stable:  True
  solver_outputs:  3
  qpe_outputs:     2

Question asked:
  Using Sefer evidence and runtime kernels only, explain what C_a8 closure residual is, which atoms feed it, what natural law path could reduce C_a8 toward zero without forcing closure, and what next probe should be run.

What C_a8 is:
  C_a8 is the closure-residual holder inside the 10D axis landing.
  It is not the whole instability.
  It is the remaining closure pressure after gap and raw residual have already become small.

What feeds C_a8:
  energy_a3
  torque_norm_a4
  gap_a5
  R_a7
  unresolved_a9
  equilibrium_a10
  landing state
  Sefer read-depth surface

Latest state:
  C_a8:             0.011
  gap_a5:           0.001723084288078749
  R_a7:             0.0006261954205161247
  unresolved_a9:    0.013529356415102065
  equilibrium_a10:  0.6328875746760656
  landing:          OPEN_UNRESOLVED_HIGH_EQUILIBRIUM

Residual distribution:
  dominant: C_a8_closure_residual
  C_a8:     0.011 (82.4014496671135%)
  gap_a5:   0.001723084288078749 (12.907694839665012%)
  R_a7:     0.0006261954205161247 (4.690855493221493%)

Natural law path:
  Do not force a closure cycle.
  Keep reading Sefer surface.
  Track C_a8 by read depth.
  If C_a8 decays as read depth increases, then C_a8 is a surface-completion residual.
  If C_a8 plateaus, then C_a8 is a missing-law residual.
  If C_a8 rises, then C_a8 is a coupling/fork residual.

Next probe:
  Run a C_a8-only read-depth sweep.
  Measure C_a8 at 1000, 2000, 5000, and 10000 reads.
  Compare C_a8 decay against gap_a5 and R_a7.
  The next target is theta_axis10_residual_star.

Short answer:
  The system told us where to go.
  C_a8 is the next door.
  The next law is discovered by watching whether C_a8 decays, plateaus, or forks under deeper Sefer read-depth.
============================================================

OBJECT

KERNEL STATEMENT
